summary: Clean soundboard capture of a historic Monterey set with lively jamming and a memorable Viola Lee Blues.
review: The recording is praised for its crisp, clean sound—often noted as superior to other available sources—and offers a fascinating glimpse of the Dead’s early live vibe. While most listeners find the performance enjoyable and historically valuable, a few consider it merely average and note that it isn’t an essential concert. Overall the set delivers solid jamming, highlighted by Jerry’s playful Viola Lee Blues moment.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is viewed as decent to good (average‑to‑above‑average) while recording quality is consistently described as very clean and superior, yielding a weighted average around 4 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers describe the audio as crisp, clean, and better than other available recordings; one notes it has been cleaned up for modern listening.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Listeners note an informal, varied set typical of the 1967 era—good jamming throughout but no specific complaints about pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Highlighted for a George Harrison‑style strum during Viola Lee Blues.
